dhp146:4: don’t you look for a lamp?
dhp147:2: a heap of festering wounds, shored up:
dhp147:3: ill, but the object of many resolves,
dhp147:4: where there is nothing lasting or sure.
dhp148:3: This putrid conglomeration is bound to break up,
dhp148:4: for life is hemmed in with death.
dhp150:3:
whose hidden treasures are:
pride & contempt,
dhp151:1: Even royal chariots well-embellished get run down,
dhp151:2: and so does the body succumb to old age.
dhp151:3: But the Dhamma of the good doesn’t succumb to old age:
dhp151:4: the good let the civilized know.
dhp153:1: Through the round of many births I roamed
dhp154:2: You will not build a house again.
dhp154:6: has attained to the end of craving.
dhp155:2: nor gaining wealth in their youth,
dhp155:3: they waste away like old herons
dhp155:4: in a dried-up lake depleted of fish.
dhp156:2: nor gaining wealth in their youth,
dhp156:3: they lie around, misfired from the bow,
